beats.text: Here's a two-by-two grid — but make it infinite. This is signal space.
Two waveforms appear: s-one and s-two, squiggly and messy. Watch them transform into arrows — vectors pointing through that space.
Gram-Schmidt kicks in. S-one gets normalised — it snaps into a clean unit arrow along the first axis, phi-one.
S-two's turn. Strip out the component pointing along phi-one. What's left gets normalised — that's phi-two, a second axis, perfectly perpendicular.
Now every signal is just coordinates. S-one lands at one point, s-two at another. Clean vectors. Zero information lost.
That's signal space. The geometric foundation of every reliable communication system.
